[![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/cesarnml/son-of-anton/pull/23)
<!-- walkthrough_start -->

## Walkthrough

This change implements the run-policy state model for delivery orchestration. New `RunPolicy` and `RunPolicyReviewSubagent` types are added to represent runtime policy configuration carrying ticket boundary mode, review policy stages, and optional review subagent overrides. Two new helpers are introduced: `deriveRunPolicyFromConfig` constructs a RunPolicy from orchestrator config, computing the reviewSubagent variant based on override presence; `normalizeRunPolicy` populates missing runPolicy on legacy states while preserving existing policy. State loading and repair workflows are updated to normalize via `normalizeRunPolicy` before returning. Comprehensive tests validate derivation, normalization, tagged-union serialization, and backward compatibility. Documentation notes the separation of pure normalization from config-dependent migration responsibilities.

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

<!-- tips_start -->

Fix only still-valid issues, skip the > rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. > > In `@docs/product/drafts/children-of-aton.md` at line 48, The CI failure is due to > the unknown word "workpad" in the docs; either replace the term "workpad" in the > draft text (e.g., use "workspace" or "working pad") wherever it appears (the > sentence starting "**GitHub Issues is the shared state layer.**" and the other > occurrence) or add "workpad" to the cspell project dictionary so the spellcheck > passes; update the draft text or the cspell dictionary accordingly and run the > spellcheck locally to confirm. > ``` > >

🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In `@tools/delivery/state.ts`: - Around line 44-45: The current guard returns early when state.runPolicy !== undefined, which treats null as present; update the normalization logic in tools/delivery/state.ts to treat both null and undefined as missing (e.g., check state.runPolicy != null or explicitly check for !== null && !== undefined) so that a persisted runPolicy: null triggers the migration/creation of a usable policy object; change the early-return condition around state.runPolicy (the block starting with if (state.runPolicy !== undefined) { return state; }) to only return when runPolicy is actually a valid policy object instead of null. --- Outside diff comments: In `@docs/product/drafts/children-of-aton.md`: - Line 48: The CI failure is due to the unknown word "workpad" in the docs; either replace the term "workpad" in the draft text (e.g., use "workspace" or "working pad") wherever it appears (the sentence starting "**GitHub Issues is the shared state layer.**" and the other occurrence) or add "workpad" to the cspell project dictionary so the spellcheck passes; update the draft text or the cspell dictionary accordingly and run the spellcheck locally to confirm. ```
🪄 Autofix (Beta) Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR: - [ ] Push a commit to this branch (recommended) - [ ] Create a new PR with the fixes
